2026-06-16-R17-brain-anti-laziness.title: Brain anti-laziness / anti-truncation prompt hardening (concept from taste-skill output-skill + laziness research)
2026-06-16-R17-brain-anti-laziness.description:
Borrow CONCEPTS ONLY (MIT, no code copy) from taste-skill's full-output-enforcement skill + its AI-laziness research to harden the VXIS Brain against premature finish / phase-skipping / truncated reasoning:
(1) Anti-shortcut directives in brain_prompts.py: 'a partial scan is a broken scan'; ban premature finish_scan; lock the expected attack-vector/phase set up front and cross-check coverage before declaring done (mirrors output-skill's scope->build->cross-check). Reinforces the existing CLAUDE.md doctrine '100% 벡터 커버리지 / Phase 건너뛰기 금지' and the finish_scan gate + coverage matrix + chain nudge.
(2) Skill-discovery win (laziness research finding: specific skill description -> 68%->90% discovery): tighten RunSkillTool / skill-card descriptions so the Brain selects the right skill instead of tripping validation. Validates the existing skill_context.py top-K design.
Concept-only; do NOT install the design skills (wrong domain — vxis is a security platform, not a frontend product).
2026-06-16-R17-brain-anti-laziness.overlap_details: vxis already has anti-laziness machinery (finish_scan gate, coverage matrix, chain nudge, verifier). This is concept-level reinforcement of the Brain prompt doctrine + a skill-description specificity win — not a new system. Design skills of the pack are NOT adopted (domain mismatch).
2026-06-16-R17-brain-anti-laziness.decision_reason: [user-approved 2026-06-16] apply option (a): persist the anti-laziness concept as a Brain prompt-hardening proposal. Concept-only (MIT). Design-skill pack NOT adopted (vxis is not a frontend product).
